subject: Physics
solution:
From the given figure, the axis of rotation is a horizontal line passing through the point $A$ on the edge of the disc. The circular arrow around the axis indicates that the disc oscillates out of its plane, meaning the axis of rotation is a tangent to the disc in its own plane.

The moment of inertia of a uniform disc about its diameter is $I_{cm} = \dfrac{1}{4}MR^2$.

Using the parallel axis theorem, the moment of inertia of the disc about the given axis of rotation is:
$I = I_{cm} + MR^2 = \dfrac{1}{4}MR^2 + MR^2 = \dfrac{5}{4}MR^2$

The distance from the center of mass of the disc to the axis of rotation is $d = R$.

The time period of a physical pendulum is given by:
$T = 2\pi\sqrt{\dfrac{I}{Mgd}}$

Substituting the values of $I$ and $d$:
$T = 2\pi\sqrt{\dfrac{\dfrac{5}{4}MR^2}{MgR}} = 2\pi\sqrt{\dfrac{5R}{4g}}$

Answer: $2\pi\sqrt{\dfrac{5R}{4g}}$

solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { Total energy }=\text { K.E. }+ \text { P.E. } \\ & \text { at } x=0.04 \mathrm{~m}, \text { T.E. }=0.5+0.4=0.9 \mathrm{~J} \\ & \begin{aligned} \text { T.E } & =1 \mathrm{~m} \omega^2 \mathrm{~A}^2=0.9 \\ & =\frac{1}{2} \times 0.2\left(2 \pi \times \frac{25}{\pi}\right)^2 \times \mathrm{A}^2=0.9 \\ \Rightarrow \mathrm{A} & =0.06 \mathrm{~m} \\ \mathrm{~A} & =6 \mathrm{~cm}\end{aligned}\end{aligned}$

subject: Physics
solution:
The given equation is $v^2 = 50 - x^2$

Differentiating with respect to $x$:

$2v \dfrac{dv}{dx} = -2x$

$v \dfrac{dv}{dx} = -x$

Since acceleration $a = v \dfrac{dv}{dx}$, we get:

$a = -x$

Comparing this with the standard equation of SHM $a = -\omega^2 x$, we get:

$\omega^2 = 1 \Rightarrow \omega = 1$ rad/s

The time period of the motion is given by:

$T = \dfrac{2\pi}{\omega} = 2\pi$

Taking $\pi = \dfrac{22}{7}$, we get:

$T = 2 \times \dfrac{22}{7} = \dfrac{44}{7}$ s

Given that the time period is $\dfrac{x}{7}$ s, comparing the two gives:

$x = 44$

Answer: $44$

In the frame of the vehicle, we have to apply pseudo force. The Force diagram is shown in figure.
Component of mg along the inclined (i.e. mgsinα) will balance the pseudo force. Therefore, geff=gcosα
               

Hence,

T=2πLgeff=2πLgcosα.

solution:
In a SHM, mechanical energy is conserved. So, the sum of kinetic energy and potential energy remains constant.

For a SHM,

T.E.-U=K.E

⇒T.E.-U=12mv2=12mω2A2-x2

This is the equation of a downward parabola.

subject: Physics
solution:
The time period of oscillation of physical pendulum is given by

T=2πIMgl  ...(1)

Where, I is the moment of inertia of ring about given axis and l is the distance of centre of mass of ring from the axis of rotation.

Moment of inertia of ring in its plane is given by

I1=Ic+ Md2=MR2+MR2=2MR2

Moment of inertia of ring perpendicular to its plane is given by

I2=Ic+ Md2=MR22+MR2=32MR2

Now the ratio of time period is given by 

T1T2=I1I2

⇒T1T2=2MR232MR2

⇒T1T2=43=23

subject: Physics
solution:
For series combination of the spring, equivalent spring constant is given by, 1keq=1k1+1k2.

Therefore, for combined system net spring constant keq=2k×k2k+k+k=5k3

Angular frequency of oscillation ω=keqm

⇒ω=5k3m

Period of oscillation τ=2πω=2π3m5k

=π12m5k

Therefore, α=12.

subject: Physics
solution:
The equation of motion is given by $x = a \sin\left(50t + \dfrac{\pi}{3}\right)$.

Velocity is the rate of change of displacement:
$v = \dfrac{dx}{dt} = 50a \cos\left(50t + \dfrac{\pi}{3}\right)$

The particle comes to rest when $v = 0$. For the first time it comes to rest:
$\cos\left(50t_1 + \dfrac{\pi}{3}\right) = 0$

$50t_1 + \dfrac{\pi}{3} = \dfrac{\pi}{2}$

$50t_1 = \dfrac{\pi}{2} - \dfrac{\pi}{3} = \dfrac{\pi}{6}$

$t_1 = \dfrac{\pi}{300} \text{ s}$

Acceleration is the rate of change of velocity:
$A = \dfrac{dv}{dt} = -2500a \sin\left(50t + \dfrac{\pi}{3}\right)$

The particle has zero acceleration when $A = 0$. For the first time it has zero acceleration:
$\sin\left(50t_2 + \dfrac{\pi}{3}\right) = 0$

$50t_2 + \dfrac{\pi}{3} = \pi$

$50t_2 = \pi - \dfrac{\pi}{3} = \dfrac{2\pi}{3}$

$t_2 = \dfrac{2\pi}{150} = \dfrac{\pi}{75} \text{ s}$

Answer: $\dfrac{\pi}{300} \, s, \dfrac{\pi}{75} \, s$

=σVg-σ16Vg =1516σVg

Time period T=2πlgeff=2πl1516g=2π1615lg=1615∙2πlg=415T
